parameters.name: td
parameters.override_behavior: sets DOUT delay after a latch event

parameters.name: vn
parameters.override_behavior: sets standard deviation of the input-referred random decision term; zero disables it

parameters.name: seed_init
parameters.override_behavior: initializes the repeatable random decision sequence
parameters.valid_range: finite integer-valued real accepted as a random seed

evaluation_summary.full_credit: valid candidate and reference pass and all five negatives killed behaviorally

modeling_constraints: Initialize rail references and the random seed at initial_step, then sample only on rising CLK midpoint crossings.
Use seeded repeatable decision noise and a smoothed supply-referenced voltage contribution for DOUT.
Do not use current contributions, ddt(), idt(), validation hooks, hard-coded waveform sample points, or simulator-specific side channels.

properties.observable_contract: The latch clock threshold is the midpoint of VDD and GND, and DOUT low and high levels use those same rails.
Each rising CLK midpoint crossing latches one comparison result; falling crossings do not resample the input.
With vn zero, DOUT latches high exactly when VINP minus VINN exceeds vos and low otherwise.
With vn nonzero, each latch decision includes a normal input-referred term scaled by vn from the sequence initialized by seed_init.
The latched decision holds between rising CLK events even if VINP or VINN changes.
DOUT applies td delay and tr transition smoothing after each latch event.

title: Latched Comparator Delay
